title: podman rootless: coredump of `fusermount -u` with SIGABRT on container exit
body:
### Issue Description

I am trying to set up my NixOS with rootless podman (or any podman to be honest), it used to work, but few months ago something changed (probably started defaulting to rootless) and it started to crash my graphical environment.

I have finally debugged the issue and what I found is that I'm always getting coredumps due to SIGABRT in `fusermount -u`  on any kind of non-root container exit (which in turn crashes my graphical environment).

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. podman --log-level=debug run --rm -it bash
2. > exit 0
3. `coredumpctl -r`


### Describe the results you received

This is user journal for run of `podman --log-level=debug run --rm -it bash`

<details>
<summary>journalctl --user logs</summary>


### Describe the results you expected

I'd not expect anything to crash on normal container operations

Why do you need to use fuseoverlay at all?  Doesn't rootless overalay work on your kernel?

To bo honest after my setup broke (didn't use it often at last few jobs) I just tried to follow https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/docs/tutorials/rootless_tutorial.md to get something working without thinking much about it.

Looks like in my case (NixOS) it is not needed at all, as the default VFS configuration works for rootless and it does rootless by default.

My only question would be: how do I force podman to run rootful when I need it to? I'm getting errors like this:
```shell
kdn@oams ~> sudo podman info
Please touch the device.
ERRO[0000] runroot must be set  
```
No idea, this works fine for Fedora.
Do you have a storage.conf somewhere on the system.

could you try upgrading FUSE?

In any case, I don't think it is a Podman issue, it is the fusermount program that is crashing.  You could try reporting to the fuse project if you don't manage to get it working after upgrading it.
